target.name: Mesh Optical Technologies
target.founder: Travis Brashears, Cameron Ramos, Serena Grown-Haeberli

methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $59M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 25.3x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Mesh Optical Technologies is a Series A semiconductor company founded by Travis Brashears, Cameron Ramos, and Serena Grown-Haeberli, having raised $58.7M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 8 private and 3 public semiconductor companies, with direct comparables including Etched ($5.0B), d-Matrix ($2.0B), Substrate ($1.0B), and Rain AI ($500M).

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x step-up multiplier to the $59M raised proxy, implying a $117M valuation. Peer-median analysis across four same-sector, same-stage companies yields a $1.5B implied valuation, with a range spanning $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via semiconductors P/S multiples (median 25.3x across ALAB, NVDA, AMD) cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure.

The two-method triangulation supports a range of $100M to $1.7B, with a median of $809M. The wide dispersion reflects material uncertainty around whether Mesh's optical technology commands frontier-sector multiples consistent with Etched and d-Matrix or tracks closer to early-stage peers like Rain AI. Key sensitivities include technical differentiation, addressable market assumptions embedded in peer valuations, and capital efficiency relative to downstream deployment timelines. Stage-progression alone suggests downside risk relative to peer medians.
